title: paeonia wittmanniana subsp. macrophylla
intro: Paeonia wittmanniana subsp. macrophylla, also known as the large-leaf paeony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the peony family (Paeoniaceae). The medicinal properties of Paeonia wittmanniana subsp. macrophylla roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause and menstrual cramps. The roots and flowers of Paeonia wittmanniana subsp. macrophylla are commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
